author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John xiv. 6, ‘I am the way, and the truth, and the life.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John xiv. 6, ‘I am the way, and the truth, and the life.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Amongst other things, when the Holy Gospel was being read, ye heard what the Lord Jesus said, “I am the Way, and the Truth, and the Life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Truth and life doth every man desire; but not every man doth find the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That God is a certain Life Eternal, Unchangeable, Intelligible, Intelligent, Wise, Making wise, some philosophers even of this world have seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The fixed, settled, unwavering truth, wherein are all the principles of all things created, they saw indeed, but afar off; they saw, but amid the error in which they were placed; and therefore what way to attain to that so great, and ineffable, and beatific a possession they formed not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that even they saw (as far as can be seen by man) the Creator by means of the creature, the Worker by His work, the Framer of the world by the world, the Apostle Paul is witness, whom Christians ought surely to believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he said when he was speaking of such; “The wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These are, as ye recognise, the words of the Apostle Paul; “The wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness, and unrighteousness of men; who detain the truth in unrighteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did he say that they do not detain truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No: but, “They detained the truth in unrighteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What they detain, is good; but wherein they detain it, is bad.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They detain the truth in unrighteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it occurred to him that it might be said to him, “Whence do these ungodly men detain the truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hath God spoken to any one of them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have they received the Law as the people of the Israelites by Moses?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence then do they detain the truth, though it be even in this unrighteousness?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what follows, and he shows.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because that which can be known of God,” he says, “is manifest in them; for God hath manifested it unto them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Manifested it unto them to whom He hath not given the Law?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear how He hath manifested it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For the invisible things of Him are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ask the world, the beauty of the heaven, the brilliancy and ordering of the stars, the sun, that sufficeth for the day, the moon, the solace of the night; ask the earth fruitful in herbs, and trees, full of animals, adorned with men; ask the sea, with how great and what kind of fishes filled; ask the air, with how great birds stocked; ask all things, and see if they do not as if it were by a language of their own make answer to thee, “God made us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These things have illustrious philosophers sought out, and by the art have come to know the Artificer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is the wrath of God revealed against this ungodliness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because they detain the truth in unrighteousness?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him come, let him show how.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how they came to know Him, he hath said already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The invisible things of Him,” that is God, “are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made; His eternal Power also and Godhead; so that they are without excuse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because that when they knew God, they glorified Him not as God, neither were thankful; but became vain in their imaginations, and their foolish heart was darkened.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the Apostle’s words, not mine: “And their foolish heart was darkened; for professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What by curious search they found, by pride they lost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Professing themselves to be wise,” attributing, that is, the gift of God to themselves, “they became fools.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the Apostle’s words, I say; “Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Show, prove their foolishness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Show, O Apostle, and as thou hast shown us whereby they were able to attain to the knowledge of God, for that “the invisible things of Him are clearly seen, being understood by those things that are made;” so now show how, “professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear; Because “they changed,” he says, “the glory of the incorruptible God into the likeness of the image of a corruptible man, and of birds, and of four-footed beasts, and of creeping things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of figures of these animals, the Pagans made themselves gods.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast found out God, and thou worshippest an idol.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast found out the truth, and this very truth dost thou detain in unrighteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what by the works of God thou hast come to know, by the works of man thou losest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast considered the universe, hast collected the order of the heaven, the earth, the sea, and all the elements; thou wilt not take heed to this, that the world is the work of God, an idol is the work of a carpenter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the carpenter as he has given the figure, could also give a heart, the carpenter would be worshipped by his own idol.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, O man, as God is thy Framer, so the idol’s framer is a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is thy God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He That made thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is the carpenter’s god?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He That made him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is the idol’s god?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that made it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then the idol had a heart, would he not worship the carpenter who made it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See in what unrighteousness they detained the truth, and found not the way that leadeth to that possession which they saw.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But Christ, for that He is with the Father, the Truth, and Life, the Word of God, of whom it is said, “The Life was the Light of men;” for that I say He is with the Father, the Truth, and Life, and we had no way whereby to go to the Truth, the Son of God, who is ever in the Father the Truth and Life, by assuming man’s nature became the Way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Walk by Him as Man, and thou comest to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By Him thou goest, to Him thou goest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look not out for any way whereby to come to Him, besides Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He had not vouchsafed to be the Way, we should have always gone astray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then became the Way Whereby thou shouldest come; I do not say to thee, seek the Way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Way Itself hath come to thee, arise and walk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Walk, with the life, not with the feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For many walk well with the feet, and with their lives walk ill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For sometimes even those who walk well, run outside the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus you will find men living well, and not Christians.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They run well; but they run not in the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The more they run, the more they go astray; because they are out of the Way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if such men as these come to the Way, and hold on the Way, O how great is their security, because they both walk well, and do not go astray!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if they do not hold on the Way, however well they walk, alas! how are they to be bewailed!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For better is it to halt in the way, than to walk on stoutly outside the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let this suffice for you, Beloved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turn we to the Lord, etc.
